name: surreal
description: SurrealDB is the ultimate cloud database for tomorrow's applications - https://surrealdb.com/

subcommands.name: help
subcommands.description: Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)

args.name: command
args.description: Command to get help for

options.description: Everything after this is an argument

name: --addr
options.description: The allowed networks for master authentication [env: ADDR=] [default: 127.0.0.1/32]

args.name: addr
args.description: The allowed networks for master authentication [env: ADDR=] [default: 127.0.0.1/32]

name: --bind
-b
options.description: The hostname or ip address to listen for connections on [env: BIND=] [default: 0.0.0.0:8000]

args.name: bind
args.description: The hostname or ip address to listen for connections on [env: BIND=] [default: 0.0.0.0:8000]

name: --key
-k
options.description: Encryption key to use for on-disk encryption [env: KEY=]

args.name: key
args.description: Encryption key to use for on-disk encryption [env: KEY=]

name: --kvs-ca
options.description: Path to the CA file used when connecting to the remote KV store [env: KVS_CA=]

args.name: kvs-ca
args.description: Path to the CA file used when connecting to the remote KV store [env: KVS_CA=]

name: --kvs-crt
options.description: Path to the certificate file used when connecting to the remote KV store [env: KVS_CRT=]

args.name: kvs-crt
args.description: Path to the certificate file used when connecting to the remote KV store [env: KVS_CRT=]

name: --kvs-key
options.description: Path to the private key file used when connecting to the remote KV store [env: KVS_KEY=]

args.name: kvs-key
args.description: Path to the private key file used when connecting to the remote KV store [env: KVS_KEY=]

name: --log
-l
options.description: The logging level for the database server [env: LOG=] [default: info] [possible values: warn, info, debug, trace, full]

args.name: log
args.description: The logging level for the database server [env: LOG=] [default: info] [possible values: warn, info, debug, trace, full]

name: --pass
-p
options.description: The master password for the database [env: PASS=]

args.name: pass
args.description: The master password for the database [env: PASS=]

name: --strict
-s
options.description: Whether strict mode is enabled on this database instance [env: STRICT=]

args.name: strict
args.description: Whether strict mode is enabled on this database instance [env: STRICT=]

name: --user
-u
options.description: The master username for the database [env: USER=] [default: root]

args.name: user
args.description: The master username for the database [env: USER=] [default: root]

name: --web-crt
options.description: Path to the certificate file for encrypted client connections [env: WEB_CRT=]

args.name: web-crt
args.description: Path to the certificate file for encrypted client connections [env: WEB_CRT=]

name: --web-key
options.description: Path to the private key file for encrypted client connections [env: WEB_KEY=]

args.name: web-key
args.description: Path to the private key file for encrypted client connections [env: WEB_KEY=]

args.name: path
args.description: Database path used for storing data [env: DB_PATH=] [default: memory]

subcommands.name: backup
subcommands.description: Backup data to or from an existing database

name: --pass
-p
options.description: Database authentication password to use when connecting [default: root]

args.name: pass
args.description: Database authentication password to use when connecting [default: root]

name: --user
-u
options.description: Database authentication username to use when connecting [default: root]

args.name: user
args.description: Database authentication username to use when connecting [default: root]

args.name: from
args.description: Path to the remote database or file from which to export

args.name: into
args.description: Path to the remote database or file into which to import
